title: Kutsaga Tobacco Varieties 2023
content:
Kutsaga Tobacco Varieties

| VARIETY | DESCRIPTION | RIPENING RATE | YIELD POTENTIAL | QUALITY | DISEASE RESISTANCE | GENERAL COMMENTS |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Kutsaga RK1 | Similar to KE1 with longer leaves | Medium to fast | Medium-high | Deep orange styles | Resistant: WM, ALT, RK | Susceptible: WF-1, TMV, ANG-1, GW, BS. Lower reapings slightly prone to guinea fowl spot. Drought tolerant. |
| Kutsaga 30R | Short plant with pale green large leaves | Slow | Medium | Deep lemon to orange | Resistant: WF-0, ALT, GW, RK, BS, TMV | Susceptible: WM, ANG-1. Prone to weather fleck if cured too fast. |
| Kutsaga 35 | Tall plant with wide crinkly leaves | Slow | High | Mostly orange-lemon | Resistant: WM, WF-0, WF-1, ANG-1, ALT, BS | Susceptible: TMV, RK, GW. Ideal for early irrigated crops. Prone to early flowering. |
| KRK 29 | Compact plant with short internodes | Very slow | High | Deep lemon to orange | Resistant: RK | Slightly prone to guinea fowl spot. |

KEY: WM=White Mould, ALT=Alternaria, RK=Root Knot Nematode, TMV=Tobacco Mosaic Virus, GW=Granville Wilt, BS=Black Shank.
